content.text: The adapter itself confirms it enables a KV-backed session driver automatically; the configured binding name has no matching KV declaration. I also found the lint configuration does not target TSX, so I’m validating the effective rules before I rank the audit.

const r = await tools.exec_command({"cmd":"for f in src/lib/utils.ts src/components/background.tsx lint-staged.config.cjs; do printf '\\n--- effective ESLint config: %s ---\\n' \"$f\"; node_modules/.bin/eslint --print-config \"$f\" 2>&1 | rg -n '\"(files|languageOptions|parser|plugins|rules)\"|@typescript-eslint|jsx-a11y|perfectionist|parserPath|Could not find|ignored|undefined' | sed -n '1,100p'; done; printf '\\n--- files missed by lint-staged glob ---\\n'; git ls-files | rg '\\.(tsx|jsx)$|\\.cjs$' | while IFS= read -r f; do case \"$f\" in *.tsx|*.jsx|*.cjs) printf '%s\\n' \"$f\";; esac; done; printf '\\n--- route input/redirect safety scan ---\\n'; rg -n --glob '!node_modules/**' '(Astro\\.params|URL\\(|request\\.url|redirect\\(|location\\.href|fetch\\(|innerHTML|set:html)' src || true; printf '\\n--- package dependency config references ---\\n'; for p in '@eslint/eslintrc' 'eslint-config-prettier' '@tailwindcss/typography'; do printf '%s: ' \"$p\"; rg -l --hidden --glob '!node_modules/**' --glob '!bun.lock' --glob '!package.json' --fixed-strings \"$p\" . | tr '\\n' ' '; printf '\\n'; done","workdir":"/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":20000});

text(r.output);

content.text:
Read-only audit complete; worktree is clean with no non-ignored files. Key findings:

1. **P1 — Cloudflare session binding is undeclared.** `astro.config.ts:9-12` sets `sessionKVBindingName: "leenk"`, while `wrangler.jsonc:1-12` has no `kv_namespaces` entry. The installed adapter explicitly enables a KV session driver and requires the binding to be declared. This can produce an invalid-binding failure.  
   Minimal fix: either remove the session setting if sessions are unused, or provision a KV namespace and declare binding `leenk` in Wrangler.

2. **P1 — TSX components receive no ESLint rules and bypass pre-commit linting.** `eslint.config.js:10` matches only `js,ts`; `eslint --print-config src/components/background.tsx` returned `undefined`. `lint-staged.config.cjs:18-21` also omits `tsx`; both tracked components are affected.  
   Minimal fix: include `tsx`/`jsx` in ESLint and lint-staged patterns, applying TypeScript and JSX-a11y rules.

3. **P1 — Deployment documentation conflicts with the configured deploy path.** `README.md:95-100` describes Cloudflare Pages Git integration, but `package.json:7` runs `wrangler deploy`, which deploys a Cloudflare Worker, and `wrangler.jsonc:3-11` is Worker configuration (`main`, `workers_dev`, `assets`).  
   Minimal fix: choose Pages or Workers as the supported target and align the README, deploy script, and configuration. [Cloudflare documents `wrangler deploy` as Worker deployment.](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/wrangler/commands/workers/)

4. **P2 — Local secret files are not portably ignored.** Repository `.gitignore:1-14` omits `.env*` and `.dev.vars*`; this machine’s global Git ignore happens to cover `.env*`, but not `.dev.vars`. Other clones can stage either.  
   Minimal fix: add `.env*`, `.dev.vars*`, and any team-approved local config patterns to the repository ignore file.

5. **P2 — Reproducible installs are ambiguous.** Only `bun.lock` is tracked, but `package.json:1-56` has no `packageManager`; `README.md:38-63` presents npm and Bun as equivalent. `npm install` can create a competing lockfile. README also permits Node 18 broadly (`:38`), while installed Astro requires `18.20.8+` (`node_modules/astro/package.json:197-200`).  
   Minimal fix: declare Bun and its version in `packageManager`, document Bun’s frozen install, and state Astro’s actual Node floor.

6. **P2 — No remote quality gate is tracked.** No `.github/workflows` files exist; the only hook is `.husky/pre-commit:1-3`, which is developer-local. `package.json:11`’s only quality command mutates files (`--fix`/`--write`), so there is no documented non-mutating CI check.  
   Minimal fix: add `lint`, `format:check`, and type/build check scripts plus a CI workflow using the chosen lockfile.

7. **P3 — Security policy and header policy need maintenance.** `SECURITY.md:11-13` provides only a non-clickable email reference and no handling expectations. No repository-managed `_headers` or Worker response-header policy is tracked; this does not prove dashboard headers are absent, but makes protections non-reviewable.  
   Minimal fix: add a `mailto:` reporting link and disclosure expectations; version-control appropriate baseline headers after accounting for the inline scripts in `src/layouts/index.astro:70-100,118-133`.

Documentation drift is also visible in `README.md:3-5,24`: badges/text claim Astro 5.1.5, TypeScript 5.3.3, and Tailwind 4.1.14, while `package.json:17-20,55` specifies newer versions.

- Verified: hard-coded redirect targets only (`src/pages/{github,linkedin,twitter}.ts:3-4`); no user-controlled redirect was found.
- Verified: no files were changed; build/format commands and live Cloudflare or registry checks were intentionally not run under the read-only scope.
